hypothesis: A known-good source tag routed through original DFII byte slot 7 should still appear if the problem is source-tag identity rather than byte-slot placement.

source_change.description: Force source slot 6 to use original write-side byte slot 7 in dfii_byte_phase_assoc_word5; leave the known v99/v103 LiteDRAM init-clean target path otherwise unchanged.

discarded_default_tdo_read.reason: Default MPSSE TDO bit returned invalid decode and was not used as DDR evidence.

source0_baseline_shape.interpretation: Consistent with the v99/v103 one-to-two phase association shape for known-good low slots.

source6_forced_through_original_slot7.result: known-good source6 tag disappeared when emitted through original byte slot 7

source7_original_slot7.result: original slot7 still produces broad non-matching nonzero data rather than the expected tag

decision.conclusion: The failure follows original write-side byte slot 7 placement, not source6/source7 tag identity.
decision.classification: write-side byte slot 7 placement/association is nonfunctional or contaminating in the current no-ODELAY LiteDRAM path.
decision.next_gate: Stop broad delay sweeps. Compare LiteDRAM/YPCB DQ-DQS-DM byte-lane grouping for original slot 7 against the generated core and either remove the bad lane from the logical data map or apply a fixed byte-slot permutation before native-port tests.
